props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: A missile repriced the barrel overnight: Brent settled Monday at $71.99 on OPEC+'s fifth straight addition, then bounced to $72.60 after Iran hit two tankers at the strait's mouth. The desk's sub-$70 Friday call is owned down to p=0.25 — the glut was sized right, the geopolitics wasn't.

The desk nearly booked a false miss: one wire's line that USD/JPY's "recent high was 162.84" dates to 1 July, before the call opened. The Bank of Japan's own sheets top this week at 162.30 — no 162.50 print. The call stays open at p=0.35 with the pair at 161.93.

Chips bounced +2.17% on Broadcom's Apple extension while S&P decliners outnumbered gainers on light volume — a narrow rally, not an all-clear. Wednesday's FOMC minutes land on September odds near 58% with ISM services prices hot at 67.7.

props.caption: The premium reloads: 61 cents of war risk in one UKMTO warning

items.what: FOMC minutes · the June hold's anatomy
items.why: September sits near 58% into Wednesday 2pm; the minutes show whether the hawkish lean was an oil story or something stickier.
Belgium won 4-1 and FIFA ruled its internal challenge inadmissible — the desk's call on a CAS filing settles Wednesday, squeezed between vindication and a closed door.

items.what: Hormuz · does a second ship burn
items.why: UKMTO's threat level and the strait's two corridors are the tell; another confirmed strike before the 14th makes the memorandum functionally dead before talks resume.
The next US-Iran round convenes after Khamenei's funeral; whether it convenes at all is now the market's cleanest read on whether the missiles were leverage or exit.
Brent's settlement and the yen's New York close print the desk's first full scoring day — one call owned down to 0.25, one saved from a false miss by primary data.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-07-07 · Foreman, Graves, Sprockett, Tinkerton

open_calls.question: ICE front-month Brent settles below $70.00
open_calls.detail: Owned down from 0.62: Monday settled $71.99 on the glut, then Iran's strikes put 61 cents and the war premium back on the barrel. The desk sized the supply correctly and the geopolitics incorrectly; Friday prints the score.

open_calls.question: USD/JPY reaches 162.50 (a retest of its 40-year low)
open_calls.detail: A wire's undated "recent high was 162.84" nearly scored this a miss — that print dates to 1 July, before the call opened. The Bank of Japan's daily sheets top the window at 162.30; the pair sits at 161.93 with intervention undisclosed and shorts still crowded.

open_calls.question: China acknowledges another east-of-Taiwan coast-guard patrol
open_calls.detail: Beijing's channels acknowledged continued patrols after the 4 July rotation, with the Xiushan and Chongming tracked 80-140 nautical miles off the east coast. The event is documented; the call scores at its deadline.

open_calls.question: Belgium files or publicly confirms a CAS action over Balogun's eligibility
open_calls.detail: Cross-currents: Belgium's 4-1 win drains the incentive, but FIFA ruling the internal challenge inadmissible leaves CAS as the only door, and UEFA's red-line statement invites the test case. Settles tomorrow.

open_calls.question: A rights-holder publicly discloses break-specific World Cup ad rates before the final
open_calls.detail: No movement; every circulating figure remains a trade estimate with a named estimator. Pricing the pause would concede the pause is priced.

items.what: ISM services held at 54.0 with the prices index at a hot 67.7, the combination that keeps September live; German factory orders rose 1.9% on the month.
SK Hynix's $28bn ADR prices into Friday as the memory cycle's referendum, one session after the chip complex's narrow rebound.
Brent's front months hold a mild contango through winter with a hump fading into February — a market that still believes in the glut just underneath the fear.
